docs.title: rnzb
docs.text: Python bindings to the nzb-rs library - a spec compliant parser for NZB files, written in Rust.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
rnzb is available on PyPI, so you can simply use pip to install it.

pip install rnzb

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from rnzb import Nzb

nzb = Nzb.from_file("big_buck_bunny.nzb")

print(f"{nzb.file.name} ({nzb.meta.category}) was posted on {nzb.file.posted_at} by {nzb.file.poster}.")
#> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv (TV) was posted on 2024-01-28 11:18:28+00:00 by John <nzb@nowhere.example>.

for file in nzb.files:
    print(file.name)
    #> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv
    #> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv.par2
    #> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv.vol00+01.par2
    #> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv.vol01+02.par2
    #> Big Buck Bunny - S01E01.mkv.vol03+04.par2

docs.title: Related projects
docs.text:
Considering this is the fourth library for parsing a file format that almost nobody cares about and lacks a formal specification, here's an overview to help you decide:

Project Description Parser Meta Editor nzb Original Python Library ✅ ✅ nzb-rs Rust port of nzb ✅ ❌ rnzb Python bindings to nzb-rs ✅ ❌ nzb-parser Javascript port of nzb ✅ ❌

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
Although nzb is already quite fast due to its use of the non-validating C-based expat parser from Python's standard library, rnzb offers even better performance, being approximately 8 times faster than nzb.

$ hyperfine --warmup 1 "python test_nzb.py" "python test_rnzb.py"
Benchmark 1: python test_nzb.py
  Time (mean ± σ):      6.306 s ±  0.075 s    [User: 5.992 s, System: 0.225 s]
  Range (min … max):    6.225 s …  6.478 s    10 runs

Benchmark 2: python test_rnzb.py
  Time (mean ± σ):     767.5 ms ±   3.4 ms    [User: 591.9 ms, System: 163.8 ms]
  Range (min … max):   762.2 ms … 772.7 ms    10 runs

Summary
  python test_rnzb.py ran
    8.22 ± 0.10 times faster than python test_nzb.py

The above benchmark was performed by looping over 10 random NZB files I had lying around. This benchmark isn't super scientific, but it gives a pretty good idea of the performance difference.

docs.title: Supported platforms
docs.text:
Refer to the following table for the platforms and Python versions for which rnzb publishes prebuilt wheels:

Platform CPython 3.9-3.13 CPython 3.13 (t) PyPy 3.9-3.10 🐧 Linux (x86_64, glibc>=2.28) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🐧 Linux (x86_64, musl>=1.2) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🐧 Linux (aarch64, glibc>=2.28) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🐧 Linux (aarch64, musl>=1.2) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🪟 Windows (x86) ✅ ✅ ❌ 🪟 Windows (x86_64) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🍏 macOS (x86_64) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🍏 macOS (arm64) ✅ ✅ ✅

The library itself is not inherently tied to any specific platform or Python version. The available wheels are based on what can be (reasonably) built using GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Building from source
docs.text:
Building from source requires the Rust toolchain and Python 3.9+.

  • With uv:
git clone https://github.com/Ravencentric/rnzb
cd rnzb
uv build
  • With pypa/build:
git clone https://github.com/Ravencentric/rnzb
cd rnzb
python -m build

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text:
Note

rnzb should only raise the errors documented on this page (currently just one). If you encounter any other errors, consider it a bug and report it.

docs.title: InvalidNzbError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised when the NZB is invalid.

docs.title: Nzb
docs.text: Represents a complete NZB file.

docs.title: file property
docs.text:
file: File

The main content file (episode, movie, etc) in the NZB. This is determined by finding the largest file in the NZB and may not always be accurate.

docs.title: filenames property
docs.text:
filenames: tuple[str, ...]

Tuple of unique file names across all the files in the NZB. May return an empty tuple if it fails to extract the name for every file.

docs.title: files instance-attribute
docs.text:
files: tuple[File, ...]

File objects representing the files included in the NZB.

docs.title: groups property
docs.text:
groups: tuple[str, ...]

Tuple of unique groups across all the files in the NZB.

docs.title: meta instance-attribute
docs.text:
meta: Meta

Optional creator-definable metadata for the contents of the NZB.

docs.title: par2_files property
docs.text:
par2_files: tuple[File, ...]

Tuple of par2 files in the NZB.

docs.title: par2_percentage property
docs.text:
par2_percentage: float

Percentage of the size of all the .par2 files relative to the total size.

docs.title: par2_size property
docs.text:
par2_size: int

Total size of all the .par2 files.

docs.title: posters property
docs.text:
posters: tuple[str, ...]

Tuple of unique posters across all the files in the NZB.

docs.title: size property
docs.text:
size: int

Total size of all the files in the NZB.

docs.title: from_file classmethod
docs.text:
from_file(nzb: str | PathLike[str]) -> Nzb

Parse the given file into an Nzb. Handles both regular and gzipped NZB files.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default nzb str | PathLike[str]

Path to the NZB file.

required

Returns:

Type Description Nzb

Object representing the parsed NZB file.

Raises:

Type Description FileNotFoundError

Raised if the specified file doesn't exist.

InvalidNzbError

Raised if the NZB is invalid.

docs.title: from_json classmethod
docs.text:
from_json(json: str) -> Nzb

Deserialize the given JSON string into an Nzb.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default json str

JSON string representing the NZB.

required

Returns:

Type Description Nzb

Object representing the parsed NZB file.

Raises:

Type Description InvalidNzbError

Raised if the NZB is invalid.

docs.title: from_str classmethod
docs.text:
from_str(nzb: str) -> Nzb

Parse the given string into an Nzb.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default nzb str

NZB string.

required

Returns:

Type Description Nzb

Object representing the parsed NZB file.

Raises:

Type Description InvalidNzbError

Raised if the NZB is invalid.

docs.title: has_extension
docs.text:
has_extension(ext: str) -> bool

Check if any file in the NZB has the specified extension.

This method ensures consistent extension comparison by normalizing the extension (removing any leading dot) and handling case-folding.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ext str

Extension to check for, with or without a leading dot (e.g., .txt or txt).

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if any file in the NZB has the specified extension, False otherwise.

```

docs.title: has_par2
docs.text:
has_par2() -> bool

Return True if there's at least one .par2 file in the NZB, False otherwise.

docs.title: has_rar
docs.text:
has_rar() -> bool

Return True if any file in the NZB is a .rar file, False otherwise.

docs.title: is_obfuscated
docs.text:
is_obfuscated() -> bool

Return True if any file in the NZB is obfuscated, False otherwise.

docs.title: is_rar
docs.text:
is_rar() -> bool

Return True if all files in the NZB are .rar files, False otherwise.

docs.text:
to_json(*, pretty: bool = False) -> str

Serialize the Nzb object into a JSON string.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default pretty bool

Whether to pretty format the JSON string.

False

Returns:

Type Description str

JSON string representing the NZB.

docs.title: Meta
docs.text: Optional creator-definable metadata for the contents of the NZB.

Category.

Passwords.

docs.title: tags instance-attribute

tags: tuple[str, ...]

Tags.

docs.title: title instance-attribute
docs.text:
title: str | None

Title.

docs.title: Segment
docs.text: One part segment of a file.

docs.title: message_id instance-attribute
docs.text:
message_id: str

Message ID of the segment.

docs.title: number instance-attribute
docs.text:
number: int

Number of the segment.

docs.title: size instance-attribute
docs.text:
size: int

Size of the segment.

docs.title: File
docs.text: Represents a complete file, consisting of segments that make up a file.

docs.title: extension property
docs.text:
extension: str | None

Extension of the file without the leading dot extracted from the File.name. May return None if it fails to extract the extension.

docs.title: groups instance-attribute
docs.text:
groups: tuple[str, ...]

Groups that reference the file.

docs.title: name property
docs.text:
name: str | None

Complete name of the file with it's extension extracted from the subject. May return None if it fails to extract the name.

docs.title: posted_at instance-attribute
docs.text:
posted_at: datetime

The date and time when the file was posted, in UTC.

docs.title: poster instance-attribute
docs.text:
poster: str

The poster of the file.

docs.title: segments instance-attribute
docs.text:
segments: tuple[Segment, ...]

Segments that make up the file.

docs.title: size property
docs.text:
size: int

Size of the file calculated from the sum of segment sizes.

docs.title: stem property
docs.text:
stem: str | None

Base name of the file without it's extension extracted from the File.name. May return None if it fails to extract the stem.

docs.title: subject instance-attribute
docs.text:
subject: str

The subject of the file.

docs.title: has_extension
docs.text:
has_extension(ext: str) -> bool

Check if the file has the specified extension.

This method ensures consistent extension comparison by normalizing the extension (removing any leading dot) and handling case-folding.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ext str

Extension to check for, with or without a leading dot (e.g., .txt or txt).

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if the file has the specified extension, False otherwise.

Examples:

>>> file.has_extension('.TXT')  # True for 'file.txt'
True
>>> file.has_extension('txt')   # Also True for 'file.txt'
True

docs.title: is_obfuscated
docs.text:
is_obfuscated() -> bool

Return True if the file is obfuscated, False otherwise.

docs.title: is_par2
docs.text:
is_par2() -> bool

Return True if the file is a .par2 file, False otherwise.

docs.title: is_rar
docs.text:
is_rar() -> bool

Return True if the file is a .rar file, False otherwise.
